revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Izanagi-jinja (Yamadahigashi, Suita)]][[ja:伊射奈岐神社 (吹田市山田東)]] {{Expand Japanese|伊射奈岐神社 (吹田市山田東)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izanagi Shrine (Q11379324) | native_name = 伊射奈岐神社 (吹田市山田東) | image = Isana y01.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Izanagi Shrine (Q11379324) | coordinates = {{coord|34.796392|135.529848}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Izanami|lt=Izanami|ar|إزانامي|az|İzanami|bcl|Izanami|bs|Izanami|ca|Izanami|da|Izanami|el|Ιζανάμι|en|Izanami|eo|Izanami|es|Izanami|fa|ایزانامی|fi|Izanami|fr|Izanami|he|איזאנמי|hr|Izanami|hu|Izanami|hy|Իզանամի|id|Izanami|it|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|ko|이자나미|mk|Изанами|mni|ꯏꯓꯥꯅꯥꯃꯤ|pt|Izanami|ru|Идзанами|simple|Izanami|sl|Izanami|sr|Изанами|sv|Izanami|ta|இசநமி|th|อิซานามิ|tl|Izanami|tr|Izanami|uk|Ідзанамі|zh|伊邪那美|qid=Q682306|1=Izanami}} }} {{nihongo|'''Izanagi Shrine'''|伊射奈岐神社 (吹田市山田東)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135009132|ltq=Q135009132|qid=Q135009132|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings}} * Province|lt=Settsu Province|ca|Província de Settsu|cs|Provincie Seccu|de|Provinz Settsu|en|Settsu Province|eo|Provinco Seccu|es|Provincia de Settsu|fa|ولایت ستسو|fr|Province de Settsu|gl|Provincia de (provincia)|sr|Провинција Сецу|tr|Settsu (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сеццу|zh_min_nan|Settu-kok|zh_yue|攝津國|zh|攝津國|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Q10976940|ltq=Q10976940|qid=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.796392|135.529848}} == image (P18) == * Isana y01.JPG == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q682306|ltq=Q682306|qid=Q682306|1=Izanami}} == described by source (P1343) == * {{ill|Q135037878|ltq=Q135037878|qid=Q135037878|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database (old)}} ** P2699: Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7120905001231 == Commons category (P373) == * Izanagi-jinja (Yamadahigashi, Suita) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Isanakino Shrine|lt=Isanakino Shrine|qid=Q135039217|1=Isanakino Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135039216|ltq=Q135039216|qid=Q135039216|1=Mishimakamono Shrine}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府吹田市山田東2丁目3-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Izanagi-jinja (Yamadahigashi, Suita)}} [[JA:伊射奈岐神社 (吹田市山田東)]] {{wikidata link|Q11379324}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine | Name=Isanaki Shrine | Image=[[File:Isana y01.JPG|270px]]
Ichi no Torii | Address={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suita City|Q653510|qid=Q653510|1=Suita}}Yamada Higashi 2-3-1 | Location={{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|47|36.6|N|135|31|57|E}} | Enshrined deity={{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Isanami Mikoto|1=Izanami}}
{{ill|lt=Amagiya Mikoto|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|lt=Amenotaji Karao|Q849412|qid=Q849412|lt=Terikio no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}
{{ill|lt=Amenoshi Kumane no Mikoto|Q91088537|qid=Q91088537|lt=Tennin Kumane no Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}
{{ill|lt=Hiruko|Q1067028|qid=Q1067028|lt=Ebisu no Mikoto|1=Hiruko}} | Company rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} (large)
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | Founded = Legend / {{ill|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235|qid=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}23 years | Main shrine = Gokensha {{ill|lt=Raguzou|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} Japanese cypress bark roof | Regular festival = {{ill|lt=May 10th|Q2557|qid=Q2557|1=May 10}} (spring regular festival)| Map= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Isanagi
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Izanagi Shrine'' (Izanagi Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Suita City|Q653510|qid=Q653510|1=Suita}} Yamada Higashi. In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, there are two ``Isanagi Shrines'' in the {{ill|lt=Shimashita District|Q10976940|qid=Q10976940|1=Shimashimo district}} section, and one of them is Engi {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi Shrine|1=Shikinaisha}}. The other troupe is said to be {{ill|lt=Izanagi Shrine (Sai-dera, Suita City)|Q11379322|qid=Q11379322|lt=a shrine of the same name|1=Izanagi Shrine (Saidera, Suita)}}, which is located in Sai-dera Temple and enshrines {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Isanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}. The old {{ill|lt=shakaku|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} is {{ill|lt=gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}.{{ill|lt=Nobutomo|Q4346747|qid=Q4346747|1=Ban Nobutomo}} Originally only {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Isanami|1=Izanami}} was enshrined here, but {{jalink|Jōkan} (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} to {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}}, it has been proven that the Isanagi god was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=solicited|1=UNKNOWN}} to our company. === History === According to the shrine's biography, in the year 23 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235|qid=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, she was the {{ill|lt=Saikū|Q150845|qid=Q150845|1=Saikū}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|qid=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. |Q11581017}}・{{ill|lt=Wahime no Mikoto|Q4388732|qid=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}}'s instruction led Okamoto Toyotahiko to search for a place to enshrine the five {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Imperial Gods|1=UNKNOWN}}, and it is said that he enshrined them in the nearby Ogawa Valley. At first it was called Himemiya Shrine. In {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}}2 ({{ill|lt=852|Q30383|qid=Q30383|1=852}}), Himemiya was moved to Mt. Takaniwa. {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jogan|1=Jōgan}} between the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}}) and the fifteenth year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=873|Q30369|qid=Q30369|1=873}}) {{ill|lt=Isanagi Shrine (Suita City Saidera)|Q11379322|qid=Q11379322|lt=Saidera Shrine|1=Izanagi Shrine (Saidera, Suita)}} {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami God|1=Izanami}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Solicit|1=UNKNOWN}}. In 873, the company name was changed from Himemiya to ``Goshagu''. {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}}``Settsushi''``Settsu Famous Places Zue'' was commonly called Gosha.The main shrine and worship hall were built in {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}13 ({{ill|lt=1636|Q6885|qid=Q6885|1=1636}}). In {{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}3), the current company name was adopted as the official name ``Osakafu Zenshi''. {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), becomes {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. {{ill|lt=1911|Q2076|qid=Q2076|1=1911}} (Meiji 44), Tsuchiyama Shrine and Tenma Shrine in Yamada no Kami, Daimyo Musa Shrine, and Tenma Shrine in Yamada Shita were merged and relocated to the precincts. Among the descendants of the first Shinto priest, Toyotahiko Okamoto, is {{ill|lt=Eitaro Okamoto|Q27920598|qid=Q27920598|1=Eitarō Okamoto}} ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Vice Minister of Agriculture and Commerce|1=UNKNOWN}}, Chairman of the Board of Directors of the Norinchukin Bank|Norinchukin Bank|Q2007236|Industrial Union Central Bank}}){{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Eitaro Okamoto (8th edition) - "Personnel Koshinroku" database |url=https://jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp/who/docs/who8-5202 |website=jahis.law.nagoya-u.ac.jp |access-date=2022-08-10}}. Served as the Japanese government representative in {{ill|lt=Geneva|Q71|qid=Q71|lt=Geneva|1=Geneva}}. === Historical evidence === {{Original research|section=1|date=April 2020}} * There are two Isanagi Shrines in Suita City ({{ill|lt=Isanagi Shrine (Saidera, Suita City)|Q11379322|qid=Q11379322|lt=Saidera Shrine|1=Izanagi Shrine (Saidera, Suita)}} and Yamada Higashi Shrine), both of which were founded in {{ill|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|Q357235|qid=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} in the year 23. The Saidera Shrine's shrine biography states, ``From {{ill|lt=Jokan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan|1=Jōgan}} to {{ill|lt=Enki|Q1374325|qid=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|qid=Q23897|1=859}} - In {{ill|lt=922|Q24219|qid=Q24219|1=922}}), {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izana Mikami|1=Izanami}} was relocated to the Tohoku region. This was called Himemiya, and the head office was called Okumiya.'' On the other hand, the biography of Yamada Tosha says, ``In the 23rd year of Emperor Yuryaku, Himemiya was founded in Ogawa Valley. Later, Himemiya was relocated to Mt. Takaniwa, and the company name was changed to Goshagu in the 15th year of the Jokan era.''* It is said that both Saidera Shrine and Yamada Higashi Shrine were awarded {{ill|lt=Shinkai|Q11591025|qid=Q11591025|1=Shinkai (divine rank)}} in the first year of Jogan (859). It is written in ``Records|Q1052981}} that ``he bestowed the rank of Junior Fifth Rank on the god Ishanagi,'' and the place that actually bestowed the divine rank was the {{ill|lt=Ishanagi Shrine, which enshrines the god Ishanagi. (Sai-dera Shrine, Suita City)|Q11379322|qid=Q11379322|lt=Sai-dera Shrine|1=Izanagi Shrine (Saidera, Suita)}} only, but not at Yamada Higashi Shrine. The Edo period Japanese scholar {{ill|lt=Nobutomo|Q4346747|qid=Q4346747|1=Ban Nobutomo}} said, ``Both Isanagi-jin and Izana-mi-jin are the ancestors of the emperor, and since Yamada Higashi-sha did not have the gift that should have been bestowed on the two shrines, Iyanagi-jinja Shrine at Yamada Higashi-sha was solicited from Saidera Shrine during the Jogan to Engi years (859 - 922).'' * However, the Osaka Prefecture Zenshi states that ``Himenomiya was relocated to Mt. Takaniwa in {{ill|lt=Ninju|Q1373077|qid=Q1373077|1=Ninju}} 2nd year (852) before Jogan,'' which suggests that Himemiya existed before Kansho. Arranged in chronological order, the company history of Yamada Higashisha is as follows: ``In the 23rd year of Emperor Yuryaku, the Himemiya was founded in Ogawadani → In the 2nd year of Jinju (852), the Himemiya was relocated to Mt. Takaba → Between the 1st year of the Jogan era and the 15th year of the Jogan era (859 - 873), the shrine of Izanami was solicited from Saidera Shrine → In the 15th year of the Jogan era, the company name was changed to Goshagu Shrine.'' Since the same deity as the enshrined deity is not prayed for, it is thought that the main deity was replaced as a result of the solicitation. It is currently unknown who the main deity that was originally enshrined at Himemiya was, or even the location of the Ogawa Valley where Himemiya was said to have existed, but its name remains in ``Yamada Ogawa Park'' located southeast of Yamada Higashisha, and a stream still flows next to the park.Near the Kashikiriyama intersection in the former downstream area, there are Okamiki Bridge and Shimookamiki Bridge (currently Yamada Bridge), which are adjacent to {{ill|lt=Okamigi Shrine|Q85878927|qid=Q85878927|1=Daishinboku Shrine}}. [[File:Okamigi Shrine Takafuda.jpg|Alternative sentence=Okamigi Shrine Takafuda|Thumbnail|Okamigi Shrine_Takafuda]] The history of Ogamigi Shrine describes how Ogamigi Shrine was founded in the 23rd year of Emperor Yuryaku in response to an oracle from {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}. It is said that while en route to the shrine of Toyouke no Okami}} from {{ill|lt=Tamba Province|Q954511|qid=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}} to {{ill|lt=Ise Province|Q864166|qid=Q864166|1=Ise Province}}, a temporary shrine was built on the local sacred tree and remained there for over a year. :'''In the course of the Sengu, {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} He built a shrine in Mishima-go Okamigi and lived there for over a year, and now he is the only Okamikigami...''' Since Okamiki-jinja Shrine, which appears in the shrine legend, was founded in the 23rd year of Emperor Yuryaku, just like Himemiya, it is likely that the temporary shrine site was once again used for rituals and became Himemiya. The Yamada Higashi-sha biography says, ``Princess Saigu Princess of Ise Jingu, Wahime (*Princess daughter of Emperor Yuryaku) Based on the teachings of {{ill|lt=Princess Chikatashihime|Q55533316|qid=Q55533316|lt=Princess Kushimatahime|1=Princess Wakatarashihime}} [also known as Princess Chikatarihime]'s teachings, Toyashihiko Okamoto (Osada no Mikoto) established a sacred place in this land where the five gods should be enshrined.Due to this, the name was changed from {{ill|lt=Yamada (Ise City)|Q11470062|qid=Q11470062|lt=Ise Yamada|1=Yamada}} and became ``{{ill|lt=Yamada (Suita City)|Q11470061|qid=Q11470061|lt=Yamadagahara|1=Yamadagahara}}.'' However, the history of this biography is somewhat skeptical due to the following points. # There is nothing in chronicles or myths that connects Ise's {{ill|lt=Yamada (Ise City)|Q11470062|qid=Q11470062|lt=Yamadagahara|1=Yamada}} with Isanagi and Izanami. # Ebisu no Kami is enshrined at Goshagu Shrine, but Ebisu worship is a later deity that began with the construction of Nishinomiya Ebisu Shrine at the end of the Heian period. # Gosha Shrine's {{ill|lt=Amagiya Mikoto|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} and {{ill|lt=Amenoshi Unne Mikoto|Q91088537|qid=Q91088537|lt=Amanin Kumane Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}} are {{ill|lt=guardian deities|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara clan|Q641628|qid=Q641628|1=Fujiwara clan}}. # In ancient times, powerful families did not worship the guardian deities of other powerful families, even if they were members of the imperial family. # In the era of Emperor Yuryaku, the {{ill|lt=Katsuragi clan|Q11620908|qid=Q11620908|1=Katsuragi clan}}, {{ill|lt=Otomo clan|Q17227938|qid=Q17227938|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}, and {{ill|lt=Kosei clan|Q11479778|qid=Q11479778|1=Kose clan}} were on the rise, and it would have been impossible to enshrine the guardian deity of the Fujiwara clan (Nakatomi clan), which was a small and powerful clan at the time, apart from these powerful clans.During the Jogan period, when Kansho was held, Inari worship, which enshrined Uka no Mitama no Kami, was very popular. Inari worship began in the Wado era (708-715) with the founding of Fushimi Inari Taisha Shrine by the Hata clan, and reached its peak during the Jogan period, when Emperor Junna granted him the rank of ``Junior Fifth Rank Lower'' in the 4th year of Tencho (827), and the highest rank of ``Shoichii'' in 5th year of Tenkei (942). Inari Daimyojin (Uka no Mitamajin) is enshrined in the set shrine of Yamada Higashisha, and it is thought that this Inari Daimyojin, not Ebisu no Kami, was originally enshrined as one of the five shrines. However, even if the Ebisu no kami of Goshagu Shrine was Uka no Mitama no Kami, it would be unreasonable to assume that Goshagu Shrine was worshiped in Ise during the era of Emperor Yuryaku. Goshagu is a Goshagu shrine when all five pillars are present, and it cannot be called a Goshagu shrine if one pillar is missing. For this reason, it is doubtful and unreliable that the other four pillars were also enshrined in Ise. It is likely that the shrine biography was altered by the Fujiwara clan after the solicitation, and the original shrine biography is ``{{ill|lt=Saikū|Q150845|qid=Q150845|1=Saikū}} Princess of Ise Grand Shrine, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Princess Chisashihime|1=UNKNOWN}}, Okamoto Toyashihiko (Osada no Mikoto) established this sacred place to enshrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyouke Okami|1=UNKNOWN}}. (Ise City)|Q11470062|Ise Yamada}} and changed its name to {{ill|lt=Yamada (Suita City)|Q11470061|qid=Q11470061|lt=Yamadagahara|1=Yamadagahara}}. The deity enshrined in Ise Yamada in Ise is Toyouke no Okami, not Izana Mikami.The name of the place {{ill|lt=Yamada (Ise City)|Q11470062|qid=Q11470062|lt=Ise Yamadagahara|1=Yamada}} was moved because the main deity of Himemiya (Karigu) was Toyouke Okami, which is {{ill|lt=Sengu|Q2271023|qid=Q2271023|1=Sengū}}; conversely, it is because the place name of Yamadagahara was moved that proves that the main deity of Himemiya is Toyouke Okami. Furthermore, ``Ogawadani,'' where the temporary shrine was located, is thought to be the current ``Shakudani.'' Shaku is a unit of length and is not related to tani. This is probably because ``Karimiya no Tani'' was mistakenly written as ``Kuramiya no Tani,'' and the abbreviation ``Kurutani'' was later rewritten as ``Shakutani.'' === Enshrined God === * Main enshrined deity - {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}} * Aidono-kami - {{ill|lt=Amagiya Mikoto|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Amenotajikarao|Q849412|qid=Q849412|lt=Terikio Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}, {{ill|lt=Amenoshi Unne Mikoto|Q91088537|qid=Q91088537|lt=Tennin Kumane Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Oshikumone}}, {{ill|lt=Hiruko|Q1067028|qid=Q1067028|lt=Ebisu no Mikoto|1=Hiruko}} === Precincts === * Main shrine (Tangible Cultural Property designated by Osaka Prefecture) - Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Kanei|Q1192913|qid=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}}13 ({{ill|lt=1636|Q6885|qid=Q6885|1=1636}}). The Gokensha {{ill|lt=Raguzou|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress bark roof is rare in Osaka Prefecture. * Worship Hall - Rebuilt in 1636. {{ill|lt=1970|Q2474|qid=Q2474|1=1970}} ({{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}}45) Renovated. * {{ill|lt=Hachimangu Shrine|Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}} - Deities enshrined: {{ill|lt=Hachiman Okami|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Hachioji Shrine|Q11391618|qid=Q11391618|lt=Hachioji Shrine|1=Hachioji Shrine}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|qid=Q382005|lt=Tenmangu|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}, {{ill|lt=Haraido Okami|Q11588504|qid=Q11588504|1=Haraedo no Kami}}. Previously, Hachimangu Shrine, Hachioji Shrine, and Tenmangu Shrine were each worshiped as separate shrines on the left side of the shrine grounds (the parking lot that is currently used as a place to store mikoshi during festivals). * Shrine office * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q818468|lt=Benzaiten|1=Benzaiten}} company * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|qid=Q514480|1=Inari shrine}} - Enshrined deity: Jumori Daimyojin === Cultural assets === === Osaka Prefecture designated tangible cultural property === * Main shrine (designated on January 14, 2011) [https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/kenzou.html Osaka Prefecture homepage/List of cultural properties designated by the prefecture (buildings)] === Tangible cultural property designated by Suita City === * Company name marker stone - Stone monument. === Suita City designated intangible folk cultural property === * Taiko Mikoshi (Yamada Isanaki Shrine Taiko Mikoshi Preservation Society) === Access === * 20 minutes walk from {{ill|lt=Hankyu Senri Line|Q1044987|qid=Q1044987|1=Hankyu Senri Line}}{{ill|lt=Yamada Station_(Osaka Prefecture)|Q3971365|qid=Q3971365|lt=Yamada Station|1=Yamada Station (Osaka)}} * {{ill|lt=Hankyu Bus|Q11656745|qid=Q11656745|1=Hankyu Bus}}3 minute walk from Yamada Miyanomae stop on Suita Line 12, 18, 21 * Hankyu Bus Suita Line 4, 12, 16, 18, 21 lines 5 minutes walk from Shin-Ogawa stop === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} === References === * Japanese historical place name system (online version) Shogakukan (database based on “Japanese historical place name system” Heibonsha, 1979-2002) === Related items === {{Commonscat|Izanagi-jinja (Yamadahigashi, Suita)}} * {{ill|lt=Isanagi Shrine (Saidera, Suita City)|Q11379322|qid=Q11379322|1=Izanagi Shrine (Saidera, Suita)}} * {{ill|lt=Yamada Village (Mishima District, Osaka Prefecture)|Q11470448|qid=Q11470448|1=Yamada}} === External link === *[https://www.izanagi-jinja.com/ Izanagi Shrine homepage] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Izanagi Shrine (Yamadahigashi, Suita)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Izanagi Shrine (Yamadahigashi, Suita)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Izanagi Shrine (Yamadahigashi, Suita)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Suita, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Suita, Osaka]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:大阪府指定有形文化財]] [[Category:山田 (吹田市)]]
